{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Copper pipe end is unreamed with a visible burr, creating a sharp internal ridge that will cause turbulence, erosion-corrosion, and noise."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work on this joint. Disassemble if already connected, ream the pipe interior to remove the burr, deburr the exterior, clean, and reassemble per manufacturer instructions."
  ],
  "rationale": "IPC Section 605 and manufacturer installation instructions require cut pipe ends to be reamed to full inside diameter and deburred. A visible burr reduces the effective inside diameter, promotes localized high velocity and erosion, and can trap debris, leading to premature failure. This is a non-compliant, defective installation that must be corrected before proceeding.",
  "references": [
    "IPC 605.2 (Preparation of pipe ends)",
    "ASTM F876 / F877 (PEX) or ASTM B88 (copper) mfr. instructions for joint preparation"
  ]
}
